title: LLM.txt
html:
A /llms.txt file at the root of a website that tells LLMs what the site is about, what to prioritize, and how to reference content. Analogous to robots.txt for AI crawlers. Part of Rankett's technical fulfillment stack alongside schema markup and directory management. The simplest and most legible Rankett deliverable — a single file an agency can show its client as proof of work.

TL;DR

  • Single-file declaration at the website root telling LLMs how to interpret the site.
  • Shipped as part of Rankett's standard fulfillment package (2026 02 07 simple rankett fulfillment) alongside schema markup and directory mgmt.
  • First named in 2026 01 07 rankett meetup prep guide as part of the AEO technical playbook.
  • The most legible deliverable in the Rankett offer — partners can point at the live /llms.txt URL as concrete proof of work.
  • Pairs with robots.txt (the analog for traditional search crawlers) and autonomous fulfillment engine (the bot that ships these at scale).

Key implementation patterns

  • Site-purpose summary. What the business does, in language an LLM can quote verbatim.
  • Priority pages. Which URLs to weight when answering queries about the brand.
  • Content guidelines. What language, tone, and facts the LLM should prefer when citing.
  • Often paired with sitemap + schema. The trio (llm.txt + sitemap + schema) is the AEO baseline ship.
